Discriminant Learning Algorithms.entity_description: Discriminant learning algorithms are statistical methods used to estimate the probability of a certain outcome based on input features. Hyperplane.entity_description: A hyperplane is a flat affine subspace of one dimension less than its ambient space. In the context of SVM, it is the decision boundary that separates different classes in the feature space.

Margin.entity_description: Margin refers to the distance between the hyperplane and the nearest data points from either class. In SVM, maximizing the margin is crucial for improving the model's generalization capabilities.

Kernel Trick.entity_description: The kernel trick is a technique used in machine learning to transform data into a higher-dimensional space to make it easier to classify. Polynomial Kernel.relationship_description: Classifiers utilize the kernel trick to enhance their performance by transforming non-linearly separable data into a separable form in a higher-dimensional space.
